title: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
summary: An F-15E Strike Eagle was lost to enemy fire and an A-10 Warthog crashed in the Persian Gulf region on Friday, officials said. A rescue helicopter was also fired upon.

title: Iran Is Quickly Repairing Missile Bunkers, U.S. Intelligence Says
summary: Reports cast doubt on how close the United States is to destroying Iran’s missile capability, a key goal in the war.

title: Ex-US Army Pilot Recalls Battle for Survival When Shot Down in Iraq
summary: Ronald Young Jr. was in the Army when his Apache Longbow copter went down during the U.S. invasion of Iraq.

title: Trump Contemplates Other Cabinet Changes as He Faces Political Clock
summary: With the midterms approaching, the president may be running out of time to get new cabinet members confirmed without bipartisan support.

title: Trump Requests $1.5 Trillion for Military Spending
summary: The huge proposed increase would be partly offset by steep cuts to domestic programs, some of which the Trump administration describes as wasteful.

title: Trump Directs Officials to Pay All D.H.S. Employees
summary: The memorandum calls for paying employees at the Coast Guard, Federal Emergency Management Agency and Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency who have gone without pay during a record-long shutdown.

title: Trump Seeks $152 Million to Begin to Turn Alcatraz Back Into a Prison
summary: The plan faces significant local political opposition and the dilapidated state of the site poses considerable logistical challenges.

title: March Jobs Report Shows Stronger U.S. Market Than Expected With 178,000 New Positions
summary: Payrolls expanded and unemployment dropped last month after a health care strike ended and a harsh winter abated.

title: Parents Who Borrowed for Children’s College Face Looming Deadline
summary: New rules mean that parent PLUS loans have to be consolidated into a new loan by June 30 for parents to keep affordable payments. But the deadline is really earlier to allow time for processing.

title: NASA Unveils 1st Earth Photos From Artemis II Moon Mission: ‘You Look Beautiful.’
summary: The pictures were released on the third day of the first mission since 1972 to send people around the moon.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Astronauts Took iPhones Into Space
summary: The astronauts traveling in the Artemis II spacecraft were allowed to take smartphones with them. Sadly, they can’t connect to the internet.

title: Artemis II Pilot Test Drove the Orion Capsule on the Way to the Moon
summary: Victor Glover, a former Navy test pilot, carefully maneuvered the Orion capsule in space around a discarded rocket stage. The demonstration is crucial for future moon landing missions.

title: Epstein Presented Himself to Indian Tycoon as a Trump White House Insider
summary: The convicted sex offender gave Anil Ambani information on appointments and foreign policy. Some seemed prescient, though there was no evidence he was close to the administration.

title: House Democrat Wages a Lonely Legal Fight Testing Congress’s Power
summary: Representative LaMonica McIver is facing crushing legal fees and prison time as she seeks to get the Justice Department assault case against her dismissed, citing her legislative prerogatives.

title: Six Senate Races to Watch as Democrats Grow Bullish Ahead of Midterm Elections
summary: It’s still a tall task for the party to win back control. Here’s the latest.

title: In the Philippines, Rising Fuel Prices Force Travelers to Stay Home During Holy Week
summary: Surging gasoline prices in the Philippines have forced some people to cancel or scale back the Visita Iglesia, a Holy Week tradition in which Catholics travel to seven churches.

title: Big Banks Seeking a Piece of SpaceX’s I.P.O. Must Subscribe to Elon Musk’s Grok
summary: Mr. Musk is requiring Wall Street firms to purchase subscriptions to his A.I. chatbot if they want to advise on one of the largest initial public offerings in history.

title: Six Flags Was a Summer Destination. Can It Win Families Back?
summary: There has never been more competition in the leisure industry, as Disney and niche parks appeal to young families and high-income visitors.

title: Is Iran Winning?
summary: The Iran expert Suzanne Maloney explains why Iran believes it has the upper hand.

title: What Trump Is Doing to the English Language
summary: There is a morality to verbs, especially in political speech.

title: Arrest of Wisconsin Mosque Leader Was Tied to Trump Antisemitism Campaign
summary: Immigration lawyers and former federal officials say the case of Salah Sarsour echoes those against other pro-Palestinian activists.

title: Stray Bullet That Killed Baby Girl in Brooklyn Also Grazed Brother
summary: Two men have been arrested in connection to a shooting on Wednesday that killed an infant. One was charged with murder, attempted murder and assault.

title: After 11 Years in Court, Heir Reclaims a Modigliani Looted by the Nazis
summary: A judge ruled against a holding company controlled by David Nahmad, the billionaire art dealer, which had bought the work at auction in 1996.

title: No One at Waffle House Remembers FEMA Official Who Says He Teleported In
summary: Gregg Phillips, who is in charge of responding to fires and floods, says the hand of God suddenly and mysteriously moved him to a 24-hour breakfast spot in Rome, Ga.
